SEARCH
Package com.societegenerale.failover.configuration

Class FailoverStoreAutoConfiguration

java.lang.Object
com.societegenerale.failover.configuration.FailoverStoreAutoConfiguration
@AutoConfiguration(after={FailoverAutoConfiguration.class,FailoverStoreMultiTenantAutoConfiguration.class}) @ConditionalOnExpression("${failover.enabled:true} eq true") @EnableConfigurationProperties(FailoverProperties.class) public class FailoverStoreAutoConfiguration extends Object
Central assembler that creates the single FailoverStore<Object> bean by combining a TenantStoreFactory (raw store) with the standard decorator chain (DefaultFailoverStore + optionally FailoverStoreAsync and/or MultiTenantFailoverStore).

Also owns all single-tenant TenantStoreFactory registrations (inmemory, caffeine, jdbc) via inner Configuration classes, keeping all store configuration in one place.

Decorator chain — four modes

Decorator chain modes
multitenant.enabledasyncChain (outermost → innermost)
false (default)true (default) FailoverStoreAsync → DefaultFailoverStore → raw
false (default)false DefaultFailoverStore → raw
truetrue (default) MultiTenantFailoverStore → (per tenant) FailoverStoreAsync → DefaultFailoverStore → raw
truefalse MultiTenantFailoverStore → (per tenant) DefaultFailoverStore → raw

MultiTenantFailoverStore is always the outermost bean in multi-tenant mode. It resolves the tenant on the calling thread before any executor boundary is crossed, then delegates to the correct per-tenant decorated store.

Author:
Anand Manissery

  • Constructor Details

    • FailoverStoreAutoConfiguration

      public FailoverStoreAutoConfiguration()

  • Method Details

    • failoverTaskExecutor

      @Bean("failoverTaskExecutor") @ConditionalOnBean(TenantStoreFactory.class) @ConditionalOnMissingBean(name="failoverTaskExecutor") @ConditionalOnProperty(prefix="failover.store", name="async", havingValue="true", matchIfMissing=true) public org.springframework.core.task.TaskExecutor failoverTaskExecutor(FailoverProperties properties)
      Default TaskExecutor for async store operations.

      Applications can override by declaring a bean named failoverTaskExecutor. Uses virtual threads when available (JDK 21+), otherwise platform threads.

      Unbounded by default. When failover.store.async-executor.concurrency-limit > 0 the virtual-thread executor is wrapped in a BoundedTaskExecutor that caps concurrently in-flight writes and applies the configured rejection-policy on overload (audit R-2); accepted tasks still run on virtual threads.

      Returns:
      virtual-thread SimpleAsyncTaskExecutor (optionally bounded) named failover-async-*

    • failoverStore

      @Bean("failoverStore") @ConditionalOnBean(TenantStoreFactory.class) @ConditionalOnMissingBean(FailoverStore.class) public FailoverStore<Object> failoverStore(TenantStoreFactory<Object> storeFactory, FailoverProperties props, @Qualifier("failoverTaskExecutor") org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<org.springframework.core.task.TaskExecutor> taskExecutorProvider, org.springframework.beans.factory.ObjectProvider<TenantResolver> tenantResolverProvider, CompositeObservablePublisher observablePublisher)
      Assembles the single failoverStore bean from the raw TenantStoreFactory and the decorator chain, driven by two properties — see the four modes in the class Javadoc.

      The chain is built in one place, reading top-to-bottom in invocation order:

      1. perTenantChain wraps a raw store in DefaultFailoverStore (defensive copy, ADR 10) and, when failover.store.async=true, in FailoverStoreAsync;
      2. when failover.store.multitenant.enabled=true, that chain becomes the per-tenant decorator inside an outermost MultiTenantFailoverStore; otherwise it is applied directly to the single-tenant raw store.

      This replaces the four property-gated bean variants with one method (the async × multitenant matrix is expressed in code, not annotations), keeping the ConditionalOnMissingBean override so consumers can still replace the whole store.

      Parameters:
      storeFactory - raw per-tenant store factory
      props - failover properties (async, multitenant, type/strategy)
      taskExecutorProvider - lazy failoverTaskExecutor; resolved only when async
      tenantResolverProvider - lazy TenantResolver; resolved only in multi-tenant mode
      observablePublisher - sink for async-failure metrics
      Returns:
      the assembled FailoverStore chain
